reasoning: The reference class is vaccine development and approval timelines for specific infectious diseases. Historically, vaccines take years to go from development to approval, and their progress is well-documented in medical news. As of February 2026, the Wikipedia page noted the vaccine status for Progressive multifocal leukoencephalopathy (PML) as 'No'. Recent searches confirm no vaccine has been developed or approved for PML or the JC virus; only theoretical/computational epitope design studies exist. The base rate of changing from 'No' to 'Yes' in a span of a few weeks without major prior news is practically zero. The only realistic way this resolves 'Yes' is if there is an uncorrected vandalism edit on the Wikipedia page exactly on the resolution dates. The likelihood of this is very low, but slightly increases with time. Thus, the probability is set to 0.02 for the near-term date and 0.03 for the later date.
